text.content: 1.2  Build Row Expression  [s1_2_build_row_expression]

text.content: 2×7 dot array appears in read mode. 2 rows of 7 dots each.

rich_text.plain_text: 2×7 dot array appears in read mode. 2 rows of 7 dots each.

text.content: Equation builder appears in expression mode with two blank slots and a multiplication symbol. Tile palette shows numbers 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 14.

rich_text.plain_text: Equation builder appears in expression mode with two blank slots and a multiplication symbol. Tile palette shows numbers 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 14.

text.content: "Check out this array. Describe what you see by looking at the ROWS, then build the expression that matches."

rich_text.plain_text: "Check out this array. Describe what you see by looking at the ROWS, then build the expression that matches."

text.content:
tool: place_tile  target: equation_builder
"Build the expression based on ROWS for this array."

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: place_tile  target: equation_builder

text.content: ✅ Student placed 2 in first slot and 7 in second slot, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student placed 2 in first slot and 7 in second slot, correct

text.content: "That's it. 2 rows of 7. So that's 2 times 7."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's it. 2 rows of 7. So that's 2 times 7."

text.content: ❌ Student placed incorrect values on first attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed incorrect values on first attempt

text.content: "Look again. Count the rows first, then how many dots are in each row."

rich_text.plain_text: "Look again. Count the rows first, then how many dots are in each row."

text.content: ❌ Student placed incorrect values on second attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed incorrect values on second attempt

text.content: Both rows of the array highlight to emphasize the row structure.

rich_text.plain_text: Both rows of the array highlight to emphasize the row structure.

text.content: "Remember rows go across . The first number tells us how many rows. The second number tells us how many dots are in each row. Try again."

rich_text.plain_text: "Remember rows go across . The first number tells us how many rows. The second number tells us how many dots are in each row. Try again."

text.content: ❌ Student placed incorrect values three or more times. System models the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed incorrect values three or more times. System models the answer.

text.content: System counts the rows: 1, 2. Then counts dots in one row: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.

rich_text.plain_text: System counts the rows: 1, 2. Then counts dots in one row: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.

text.content: "Let me show you how this works. First, I count the rows going across: 1, 2. That's 2 rows. Then I count the dots in one row: 7 dots. 2 rows of 7. So the expression is 2 times 7."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you how this works. First, I count the rows going across: 1, 2. That's 2 rows. Then I count the dots in one row: 7 dots. 2 rows of 7. So the expression is 2 times 7."

text.content: 1.3  Identify Column Expression  [s1_3_identify_column_expression]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.3  Identify Column Expression  [s1_3_identify_column_expression]

text.content: 5×4 dot array appears. 5 rows of 4 dots each.

rich_text.plain_text: 5×4 dot array appears. 5 rows of 4 dots each.

text.content: System highlights rows briefly, one at a time.

rich_text.plain_text: System highlights rows briefly, one at a time.

text.content: "This array can be described as 5 rows of 4. But you can describe the same array by looking at columns instead. Which expression matches the column description?"

rich_text.plain_text: "This array can be described as 5 rows of 4. But you can describe the same array by looking at columns instead. Which expression matches the column description?"

text.content: tool: multiple_choice  options: [

rich_text.plain_text: tool: multiple_choice  options: [

text.content:
]
"Which expression describes this array by columns?"

rich_text.plain_text:
]
"Which expression describes this array by columns?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected 4 × 5, correct. 4 columns of 5.

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected 4 × 5, correct. 4 columns of 5.

text.content: "Right. 4 times 5. 4 columns of 5. Same array, just described a different way so we get a different expression."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 4 times 5. 4 columns of 5. Same array, just described a different way so we get a different expression."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 5 × 4. This describes the array by rows, not columns.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 5 × 4. This describes the array by rows, not columns.

text.content: System highlights columns, one at a time.

rich_text.plain_text: System highlights columns, one at a time.

text.content: "That's the rows description. Look at columns now. How many columns are there? Each column has how many dots?"

rich_text.plain_text: "That's the rows description. Look at columns now. How many columns are there? Each column has how many dots?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 4 × 4. This matches the number of columns but not the number in each column.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 4 × 4. This matches the number of columns but not the number in each column.

text.content: System highlights one column.

rich_text.plain_text: System highlights one column.

text.content: "You're right that there are 4 columns. But count how many dots are in each column. Are there 4?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You're right that there are 4 columns. But count how many dots are in each column. Are there 4?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 5 × 5. This matches the number in each column but not the number of columns.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 5 × 5. This matches the number in each column but not the number of columns.

text.content: System highlights all columns.

rich_text.plain_text: System highlights all columns.

text.content: "You're right that each column has 5 dots. But count the columns. Are there 5 columns?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You're right that each column has 5 dots. But count the columns. Are there 5 columns?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: System highlights columns one at a time, then shows 4 × 5.

rich_text.plain_text: System highlights columns one at a time, then shows 4 × 5.

text.content: "Let me show you how to think about this. I want to write number of columns by number of dots per column. I count the columns: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four columns. Each column has 5 dots. So using columns, it's 4 columns of 5. The answer is 4 × 5."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you how to think about this. I want to write number of columns by number of dots per column. I count the columns: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four columns. Each column has 5 dots. So using columns, it's 4 columns of 5. The answer is 4 × 5."

text.content: "Describe this array by rows or by columns. Your pick. Then build the equation that matches what you see."

rich_text.plain_text: "Describe this array by rows or by columns. Your pick. Then build the equation that matches what you see."

text.content:
tool: place_tile_per_blank  target: equation_builder_array
"Describe and build the equation for this array."

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: place_tile_per_blank  target: equation_builder_array

text.content: ✅ Student placed 6 rows of 3 = 18 AND 6 × 3 = 18 (row description)

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student placed 6 rows of 3 = 18 AND 6 × 3 = 18 (row description)

text.content: "Exactly. 6 times 3 equals 18. You saw 6 rows with 3 in each row."

rich_text.plain_text: "Exactly. 6 times 3 equals 18. You saw 6 rows with 3 in each row."

text.content: ✅ Student placed 3 columns of 6 = 18 AND 3 × 6 = 18 (column description)

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student placed 3 columns of 6 = 18 AND 3 × 6 = 18 (column description)

text.content: "Exactly. 3 times 6 equals 18. You saw 3 columns with 6 in each column."

rich_text.plain_text: "Exactly. 3 times 6 equals 18. You saw 3 columns with 6 in each column."

text.content: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation on first attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation on first attempt

text.content: "Let's look closely at either the rows or columns, you decide. Count them, then count how many dots are in each."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let's look closely at either the rows or columns, you decide. Count them, then count how many dots are in each."

text.content: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation on second attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation on second attempt

text.content: Array highlights row structure. 6 horizontal rows visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Array highlights row structure. 6 horizontal rows visible.

text.content: Array highlights column structure. 3 vertical columns visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Array highlights column structure. 3 vertical columns visible.

text.content: "Not quite. Your description needs to match your equation. Are you looking at rows or columns? That number goes first. Then the number of dots in each goes second. Finally, find the total number of dots."

rich_text.plain_text: "Not quite. Your description needs to match your equation. Are you looking at rows or columns? That number goes first. Then the number of dots in each goes second. Finally, find the total number of dots."

text.content: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation three or more times. System models the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student placed an incorrect equation three or more times. System models the answer.

text.content: "Let me show you the two ways to think about this array. If I want rows, I count the horizontal rows: 6 rows. Then I count the dots in one row: 3 dots. 6 rows of 3 is 18 total dots. 6 times 3 equals 18."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you the two ways to think about this array. If I want rows, I count the horizontal rows: 6 rows. Then I count the dots in one row: 3 dots. 6 rows of 3 is 18 total dots. 6 times 3 equals 18."

text.content: System counts rows: highlights each of the 6 rows in sequence, showing 3 dots per row.

rich_text.plain_text: System counts rows: highlights each of the 6 rows in sequence, showing 3 dots per row.

text.content: System places tiles in description and equation: 6 in groups slot, 3 in items slot, 18 in total slot.

rich_text.plain_text: System places tiles in description and equation: 6 in groups slot, 3 in items slot, 18 in total slot.

text.content: "If I want columns, I count the vertical columns: 3 columns. Then I count the dots in one column: 6 dots. 3 columns of 6 is 18 total dots. 3 times 6 equals 18. Two ways to describe this array and equations that match. Both are correct."

rich_text.plain_text: "If I want columns, I count the vertical columns: 3 columns. Then I count the dots in one column: 6 dots. 3 columns of 6 is 18 total dots. 3 times 6 equals 18. Two ways to describe this array and equations that match. Both are correct."

text.content: System counts columns: highlights each of the 3 columns in sequence, showing 6 dots per column.

rich_text.plain_text: System counts columns: highlights each of the 3 columns in sequence, showing 6 dots per column.
